subsection.heading: In general
subsection.chapeau: This chapter does not apply to credit transactions involving extensions of credit—

paragraph.content: primarily for business, commercial, or agricultural purposes; or
to government or governmental agencies or instrumentalities.

content.p: In prescribing regulations under section 2617(a) of this title, the Bureau shall ensure that, with respect to subsection (a) of this section, the exemption for credit transactions involving extensions of credit primarily for business, commercial, or agricultural purposes, as provided in subsection (a)(1) 11 See References in Text note below. of this section shall be the same as the exemption for such credit transactions under section 1603(1) of title 15.

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: Subsection (a)(1) of this section, referred to in subsec. (b), was in the original “section 7(1) of the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act of 1974”, and was translated as referring to section 7(a)(1) of that Act to reflect the probable intent of Congress.

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 2606, Pub. L. 93–533, § 7, Dec. 22, 1974, 88 Stat. 1727, related to seller or his agent confirming that information concerning an existing residence was disclosed to buyer in writing before a commitment for a mortgage loan was made, prior to repeal by Pub. L. 94–205, § 6, Jan. 2, 1976, 89 Stat. 1158.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1996—Pub. L. 104–208 designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, and added subsec. (b).

note.heading: Effective Date of 2010 Amendment
note.p: Amendment by Pub. L. 111–203 effective on the designated transfer date, see section 1100H of Pub. L. 111–203, set out as a note under section 552a of Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.
